[package]

[workspace]

--- src/errors.rs ---
// ---- GENERATED BY QEDGEN — DO NOT EDIT ---- spec-hash:a0967c28d639880e
use anchor_lang::prelude::*;

#[error_code]
pub enum PoolDemoError {
    InvalidAmount = 0,
    WrongState = 1,
    InvalidLifecycle = 2,
}
// ---- END GENERATED ----

--- src/guards.rs ---
// ---- GENERATED BY QEDGEN — DO NOT EDIT — regenerated from .qedspec ----
//! Per-handler guard checks derived from the `.qedspec`.
//! Called from user-owned `instructions/<name>::handler` before
//! business logic; keep guard logic here, policy-free logic there.

#![allow(unused_variables, unused_imports, dead_code, clippy::too_many_arguments)]

use anchor_lang::prelude::*;
use crate::errors::*;
use crate::state::*;
use crate::*;

/// Guards for `initialize`.  
/// Generated from the `requires` clauses of the spec handler block.
pub fn initialize(ctx: &Initialize, initial: u64) -> Result<()> {
    // requires: initial > 0
    if !(initial > 0) { return Err(PoolDemoError::InvalidAmount.into()); }
    Ok(())
}

/// Guards for `deposit`.  
/// Generated from the `requires` clauses of the spec handler block.
pub fn deposit(ctx: &Deposit, amount: u64) -> Result<()> {
    // requires: amount > 0
    if !(amount > 0) { return Err(PoolDemoError::InvalidAmount.into()); }
    Ok(())
}

// ---- END GENERATED ----

--- src/instructions/deposit.rs ---
// User-owned. Regenerating the spec does NOT overwrite this file.
// Guard checks live in the sibling `crate::guards` module and ARE
// regenerated on every `qedgen codegen`. Drift between the spec
// handler block and the `spec_hash` below fires a compile_error!
// via the `#[qed(verified, ...)]` macro.

use anchor_lang::prelude::*;
use crate::guards;
use qedgen_macros::qed;
use crate::errors::*;
use crate::state::PoolDemoAccountInner;
use crate::Deposit;

impl Deposit {
    #[qed(verified, spec = "../pool.qedspec", handler = "deposit", hash = "b99d8daf7723f6e9", spec_hash = "8ce43ce26e3bb59e")]
    #[inline(always)]
    pub fn handler(&self, amount: u64) -> Result<()> {
        guards::deposit(self, amount)?;
        // Spec effect (needs fill): pool_balance add amount
        // Spec call: Token.transfer(amount=amount) — v2.9 will emit a generic Anchor CPI
        todo!("fill non-mechanical effects, events, transfers, calls")
    }
}

--- src/instructions/initialize.rs ---
// User-owned. Regenerating the spec does NOT overwrite this file.
// Guard checks live in the sibling `crate::guards` module and ARE
// regenerated on every `qedgen codegen`. Drift between the spec
// handler block and the `spec_hash` below fires a compile_error!
// via the `#[qed(verified, ...)]` macro.

use anchor_lang::prelude::*;
use crate::guards;
use qedgen_macros::qed;
use crate::state::PoolDemoAccountInner;
use crate::Initialize;

impl Initialize {
    #[qed(verified, spec = "../pool.qedspec", handler = "initialize", hash = "573e6946bf2ddb65", spec_hash = "72630c878c5720f8")]
    #[inline(always)]
    pub fn handler(&self, initial: u64) -> Result<()> {
        guards::initialize(self, initial)?;
        // Spec effect (needs fill): pool_balance set initial
        todo!("fill non-mechanical effects, events, transfers, calls")
    }
}

--- src/instructions/mod.rs ---
// ---- GENERATED BY QEDGEN — DO NOT EDIT ---- spec-hash:6611bab7cd6f24d3

--- src/lib.rs ---
// ---- GENERATED BY QEDGEN — DO NOT EDIT ---- spec-hash:798efe9c54ed36fb
#![allow(unexpected_cfgs)]

use anchor_lang::prelude::*;

mod instructions;
pub mod errors;
pub mod state;
pub mod guards;

declare_id!("11111111111111111111111111111111");

#[program]
pub mod pooldemo {
    use super::*;

    pub fn initialize(ctx: Context<Initialize>, initial: u64) -> Result<()> {
        ctx.accounts.handler(initial)
    }

    pub fn deposit(ctx: Context<Deposit>, amount: u64) -> Result<()> {
        ctx.accounts.handler(amount)
    }

}

// `#[derive(Accounts)]` structs live at the crate root so the
// Anchor `#[program]` macro can resolve them via `crate::*`.
// The handler impl blocks live next to the (always-regenerated)
// guard module in `instructions/<name>.rs`.
use crate::state::*;

#[derive(Accounts)]
pub struct Initialize {
}

#[derive(Accounts)]
pub struct Deposit {
}
// ---- END GENERATED ----

--- src/state.rs ---
// ---- GENERATED BY QEDGEN — DO NOT EDIT ---- spec-hash:c2fd96777ac0a20b
use anchor_lang::prelude::*;

#[account]
#[derive(InitSpace)]
pub struct PoolDemoAccount {
    pub inner: PoolDemoAccountInner,
}

/// Variant-payload state for PoolDemoAccount. The Anchor wrapper above
/// carries the account discriminator; this enum carries the
/// state-machine variant + per-variant payload fields.
#[derive(AnchorSerialize, AnchorDeserialize, InitSpace, Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub enum PoolDemoAccountInner {
    Uninitialized,
    Open {
        pool_balance: u64,
    },
}

impl PoolDemoAccountInner {
    /// v2.29 Slice B accessor for `pool_balance`. Panics on variants
/// that don't carry the field — guarded against by the
/// per-handler lifecycle check that fires before any
/// `requires` emission in `crate::guards`.
    pub fn pool_balance(&self) -> &u64 {
        match self {
            Self::Open { pool_balance, .. } => pool_balance,
            _ => panic!("PoolDemoAccountInner::pool_balance() called on a variant without `pool_balance`"),
        }
    }
}
// ---- END GENERATED ----
